frameworks.name: mixture model of visual working memory
frameworks.one_line: Decomposes recall errors into precision and guessing components

frameworks.name: exogenous vs endogenous attention
frameworks.one_line: Extends retro-cueing literature from voluntary to involuntary attention

frameworks.name: retro-cueing of working memory
frameworks.one_line: Post-stimulus attention cues modulate stored memory representations

core_question: Do uninformative exogenous retro-cues modulate visual working memory performance, and if so, via which error component?
key_assumptions: Recall errors decompose into precision vs guess-rate components
Uninformative cues engage exogenous, not endogenous, attention
Attention can act on memory representations after stimuli disappear
manipulations: Cue timing relative to stimulus offset (retro-cue)
Delayed estimation report of remembered feature
key_findings: Uninformative exogenous retro-cues improved visual working memory performance
Improvement appeared specifically in the precision component of recall
interpretations: Memory precision is a flexible trait modulated even by involuntary attention
Attention-memory link extends to exogenous cueing applied after stimulus offset
